mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following rate cuts are negative in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate above a 0.2 percentage point threshold over three months signals deteriorating labor demand and recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand strength, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative rank IC over any 24-month rolling period, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly expansions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side capacity utilization and corporate output growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive industrial production revisions are negative in >58% of cases during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 255 while the federal funds rate remains above 2.0 percent, real rates turn negative and monetary policy tightens, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined threshold produces positive 20-day returns in >55% of observations across a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns following payroll gains are negative in >50% of cases over a rolling 36-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent deceleration in real GDP growth below 2.0 percent and monthly retail sales declines signal a demand contraction that triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 2.0% and RSAFS declines
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of instances when both GDP and retail sales deteriorate simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds 108, it signals peak capacity utilization and strong manufacturing output, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where the industrial production index surpasses the 108 threshold.
